message:
fix: skip WBQ enqueue for reservation releases with no archive-eligible rows

Move the WBQ FileReservation enqueue into an `else` branch so it only
fires when the cleanup actually produced artifacts worth archiving.
Previously the enqueue ran unconditionally — even immediately after
logging that zero rows were eligible — wasting a write-back queue slot
and persisting an empty payload.

Also applies two minor rustfmt reformats in the reservation release
test (line-wrapping for `released_ids` and `artifact_path`).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
